title: Local Networking, Name Resolution, and Secure Remote Access · Operations Case: Connectivity Sequence
summary: Restore access to a host by testing local configuration, route, name resolution, listening sockets, and SSH trust in causal order. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux connectivity sequence increment in a layered connectivity and SSH trust runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
Trace hosts files, resolver configuration, search domains, DNS queries, caches, and application lookup results.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic stateful terminal simulator.
# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

steps.title: Predict Connectivity Sequence
steps.instruction:
theory case — Host has 192.0.2.40/24, default route 192.0.2.1, DNS 192.0.2.53, and service port 443. Name lookup succeeds while the socket is absent; test layers and preserve SSH host-key trust.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Connectivity Sequence contract for the supplied data.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for test local process, local socket, route, name resolution, transport, and application protocol with the smallest useful probe.
